channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: ECJ rejects Karić’s appeal against judgment refusing his Belarus de-listing application
item.description: Karić v Council - Case C‑39/25 P. The European Court of Justice has rejected Bogoljub Karić’s appeal against General Court judgment dismissing his application to annul his 2022 designation and 2023 re

item.title: EU renews Lebanon sanctions regime until July 2027
item.description: The EU has renewed its Regulation (EU) 2021/1275 Lebanon sanctions regime until 31 July 2027. The Reg 1275 regime imposes autonomous EU sanctions on people and entities linked to Lebanon in addition t

item.title: UK publishes FAQ guidance on basic needs allowance licences
item.description: The UK has added 6 FAQs on basic needs allowance (BNA) licences to its frequently asked questions. A BNA licence is a specific licence available across all non-counter-terrorism sanctions regimes.

item.title: US imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods under Section 338 Tariff Act 1930
item.description: President Trump signed 3 proclamations imposing an additional 50% ad valorem duty on certain Canadian goods – White House fact sheet. The tariffs were made under Section 338 of the Tariff Act of 1930,

item.title: EU updates FAQ guidance on meaning of “operator” in Reg 833 following CJEU preliminary ruling
item.description: The EU has updated its FAQ guidance on the meaning of operator under Article 2f of Regulation (EU) 833/2014 following the Court of Justice of the EU’s judgment in the Traugott Ickeroth preliminary rul

item.title: Norway amends sanctions regulation to align with EU’s 20th sanctions package
item.description: Norway has introduced a new sanctions package against Russia, to align its domestic law with the EU’s 20th sanctions package - Press release. The sanctions package was introduced through a regulation

item.title: EU General Court rejects Beloglazov’s Russia de-listing case & interprets “facilitating sanctions circumvention”
item.description: Beloglazov v Council (T-492/24). The EU General Court has rejected Dimitry Beloglazov’s application to annul his designation under Article 3(1)(h)(i) of Regulation (EU) 269/2014 for facilitating the c

item.title: Dutch bank fined for inadequate sanctions and anti-money laundering controls
item.description: De Nederlandsche Bank has imposed an administrative fine of €8.5 million on ABN AMRO Bank operating under deficient anti-money laundering and sanctions controls between September 2023 and 2024. ABN

item.title: European Public Prosecutor’s Office requests investigation into sanctions circumvention in Poland
item.description: The European Public Prosecutor’s Office (EPPO) has requested law enforcement authorities in Poland to investigate a case of suspected circumvention of Russia sanctions. The suspect is suspected of hav

item.title: EU statement on third country alignment with chemical weapons designations
item.description: The EU has issued a statement on third country alignment with EU designations against the proliferation and use of chemical weapons. On 3 July 2026, 6 people said to be linked to the death of Alexei N
